theme: scribe editing a scroll that never leaves the desk
title: Cursor-Biased Scroll: Finger Anchors Across Jump Epochs
strategies: do-nothing baseline: never install a bookmark, service every touch from the fixed central spindle (checker's own reference construction)

uniform-window greedy: split the M touches into F equal-sized chronological chunks and drop one bookmark at each chunk's median position at the chunk's start -- the obvious 'use my whole finger budget evenly' recipe, blind to where the real epoch boundaries and revisited hubs actually are

changepoint segmentation: detect genuine jump epochs from the trace itself (a robust local-step threshold), merge/reuse bookmarks for epochs that revisit a nearby earlier hub instead of relocating, and only pay the linear relocation cost for a segment when its length times the log-cost savings exceeds that cost -- reframing anchor placement as a segmentation + cost-benefit decision instead of a fixed schedule
